This should\n// only be used for \"global\" lookups, such as initiating a new push to all proxies.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) globalPushContext() *model.PushContext {\n\treturn s.Env.PushContext()\n}\n\nvar fullPushLog = istiolog.RegisterScope(\"fullpush\", \"logs details about why Istio is triggering a full push\")\n\n// ConfigUpdate implements ConfigUpdater interface, used to request pushes.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) ConfigUpdate(req *model.PushRequest) {\n\tif features.EnableUnsafeAssertions {\n\t\tif model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Service) {\n\t\t\tpanic(\"assertion failed kind.Service can not be set in ConfigKey\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Address) {\n\t\t// This is a bit like clearing EDS cache on EndpointShard update. Because Address\n\t\t// types are fetched dynamically, they are not part of the same protections, so we need to clear\n\t\t// the cache.\n\t\ts.Cache.ClearAll()\n\t}\n\tinboundConfigUpdates.Increment()\n\ts.InboundUpdates.Inc()\n\tif req.Full && fullPushLog.DebugEnabled() {\n\t\tconfigs := slices.Sort(slices.Map(req.ConfigsUpdated.UnsortedList(), model.ConfigKey.String))\n\t\treasons := maps.Keys(req.Reason)\n\t\tfullPushLog.Debugf(\"full push triggered configs=%v reasons=%v\", configs, reasons)\n\t}\n\ts.pushChannel <- req\n}\n\n// Debouncing and push request happens in a separate thread, it uses locks\n// and we want to avoid complications, ConfigUpdate may already hold other locks.\n// handleUpdates processes events from pushChannel\n// It ensures that at minimum minQuiet time has elapsed since the last event before processing it.\n// It also ensures that at most maxDelay is elapsed between receiving an event and processing it.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) handleUpdates(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\n\tdebounce(s.pushChannel, stopCh, s.DebounceOptions, s.Push, s.CommittedUpdates)\n}\n\n// The debounce helper function is implemented to enable mocking\nfunc debounce(ch chan *model.PushRequest, stopCh <-chan struct{}, opts DebounceOptions, pushFn func(req *model.PushRequest), updateSent *atomic.Int64) {\n\tvar timeChan <-chan time.Time\n\tvar startDebounce time.Time\n\tvar lastConfigUpdateTime time.Time\n\n\tpushCounter := 0\n\tdebouncedEvents := 0\n\n\t// Keeps track of the push requests. If updates are debounce they will be merged.\n\tvar req *model.PushRequest\n\n\tfree := true\n\tfreeCh := make(chan struct{}, 1)\n\n\tpush := func(req *model.PushRequest, debouncedEvents int, startDebounce time.Time) {\n\t\tpushFn(req)\n\t\tupdateSent.Add(int64(debouncedEvents))\n\t\tdebounceTime.Record(time.Since(startDebounce).Seconds())\n\t\tfreeCh <- struct{}{}\n\t}\n\n\tpushWorker := func() {\n\t\teventDelay := time.Since(startDebounce)\n\t\tquietTime := time.Since(lastConfigUpdateTime)\n\t\t// it has been too long or quiet enough\n\t\tif eventDelay >= opts.debounceMax || quietTime >= opts.DebounceAfter {\n\t\t\tif req != nil {\n\t\t\t\tpushCounter++\n\t\t\t\tif req.ConfigsUpdated == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"Push debounce stable[%d] %d for reason %s: %v since last change, %v since last push, full=%v\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tpushCounter, debouncedEvents, reasonsUpdated(req),\n\t\t\t\t\t\tquietTime, eventDelay, req.Full)\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"Push debounce stable[%d] %d for config %s: %v since last change, %v since last push, full=%v\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tpushCounter, debouncedEvents, configsUpdated(req),\n\t\t\t\t\t\tquietTime, eventDelay, req.Full)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tfree = false\n\t\t\t\tgo push(req, debouncedEvents, startDebounce)\n\t\t\t\treq = nil\n\t\t\t\tdebouncedEvents = 0\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\ttimeChan = time.After(opts.DebounceAfter - quietTime)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tfor {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-freeCh:\n\t\t\tfree = true\n\t\t\tpushWorker()\n\t\tcase r := <-ch:\n\t\t\t// If reason is not set, record it as an unknown reason\n\t\t\tif len(r.Reason) == 0 {\n\t\t\t\tr.Reason = model.NewReasonStats(model.UnknownTrigger)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif !opts.enableEDSDebounce && !r.Full {\n\t\t\t\t// trigger push now, just for EDS\n\t\t\t\tgo func(req *model.PushRequest) {\n\t\t\t\t\tpushFn(req)\n\t\t\t\t\tupdateSent.Inc()\n\t\t\t\t}(r)\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tlastConfigUpdateTime = time.Now()\n\t\t\tif debouncedEvents == 0 {\n\t\t\t\ttimeChan = time.After(opts.DebounceAfter)\n\t\t\t\tstartDebounce = lastConfigUpdateTime\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tdebouncedEvents++\n\n\t\t\treq = req.Merge(r)\n\t\tcase <-timeChan:\n\t\t\tif free {\n\t\t\t\tpushWorker()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tcase <-stopCh:\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc configsUpdated(req *model.PushRequest) string {\n\tconfigs := \"\"\n\tfor key := range req.ConfigsUpdated {\n\t\tconfigs += key.String()\n\t\tbreak\n\t}\n\tif len(req.ConfigsUpdated) > 1 {\n\t\tmore := \" and \" + strconv.Itoa(len(req.ConfigsUpdated)-1) + \" more configs\"\n\t\tconfigs += more\n\t}\n\treturn configs\n}\n\nfunc reasonsUpdated(req *model.PushRequest) string {\n\tvar (\n\t\treason0, reason1            model.TriggerReason\n\t\treason0Cnt, reason1Cnt, idx int\n\t)\n\tfor r, cnt := range req.Reason {\n\t\tif idx == 0 {\n\t\t\treason0, reason0Cnt = r, cnt\n\t\t} else if idx == 1 {\n\t\t\treason1, reason1Cnt = r, cnt\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t\tidx++\n\t}\n\n\tswitch len(req.Reason) {\n\tcase 0:\n\t\treturn \"unknown\"\n\tcase 1:\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d\", reason0, reason0Cnt)\n\tcase 2:\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d and %s:%d\", reason0, reason0Cnt, reason1, reason1Cnt)\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d and %d(%d) more reasons\", reason0, reason0Cnt, len(req.Reason)-1,\n\t\t\treq.Reason.Count()-reason0Cnt)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc doSendPushes(stopCh <-chan struct{}, semaphore chan struct{}, queue *PushQueue) {\n\tfor {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-stopCh:\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t// We can send to it until it is full, then it will block until a pushes finishes and reads from it.\n\t\t\t// This limits the number of pushes that can happen concurrently\n\t\t\tsemaphore <- struct{}{}\n\n\t\t\t// Get the next proxy to push. This will block if there are no updates required.\n\t\t\tclient, push, shuttingdown := queue.Dequeue()\n\t\t\tif shuttingdown {\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\trecordPushTriggers(push.Reason)\n\t\t\t// Signals that a push is done by reading from the semaphore, allowing another send on it.\n\t\t\tdoneFunc := func() {\n\t\t\t\tqueue.MarkDone(client)\n\t\t\t\t<-semaphore\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tproxiesQueueTime.Record(time.Since(push.Start).Seconds())\n\t\t\tvar closed <-chan struct{}\n\t\t\tif client.deltaStream != nil {\n\t\t\t\tclosed = client.deltaStream.Context().Done()\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tclosed = client.StreamDone()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tgo func() {\n\t\t\t\tpushEv := &Event{\n\t\t\t\t\tpushRequest: push,\n\t\t\t\t\tdone:        doneFunc,\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\t\tcase client.PushCh() <- pushEv:\n\t\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t\tcase <-closed: // grpc stream was closed\n\t\t\t\t\tdoneFunc()\n\t\t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"Client closed connection %v\", client.ID())\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}()\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// initPushContext creates a global push context and stores it on the environment. Note: while this\n// method is technically thread safe (there are no data races), it should not be called in parallel;\n// if it is, then we may start two push context creations (say A, and B), but then write them in\n// reverse order, leaving us with a final version of A, which may be incomplete.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) initPushContext(req *model.PushRequest, oldPushContext *model.PushContext, version string) *model.PushContext {\n\tpush := model.NewPushContext()\n\tpush.PushVersion = version\n\tpush.JwtKeyResolver = s.JwtKeyResolver\n\tpush.InitContext(s.Env, oldPushContext, req)\n\n\ts.dropCacheForRequest(req)\n\ts.Env.SetPushContext(push)\n\n\treturn push\n}\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) sendPushes(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\n\tdoSendPushes(stopCh, s.concurrentPushLimit, s.pushQueue)\n}\n\n// Shutdown shuts down DiscoveryServer components.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) Shutdown() {\n\ts.closeJwksResolver()\n\ts.pushQueue.ShutDown()\n}\n\n// Clients returns all currently connected clients. This method can be safely called concurrently,\n// but care should be taken with the underlying objects (ie model.Proxy) to ensure proper locking.\n// This method returns only fully initialized connections; for all connections, use AllClients\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) Clients() []*Connection {\n\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\n\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\n\tclients := make([]*Connection, 0, len(s.adsClients))\n\tfor _, con := range s.adsClients {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-con.InitializedCh():\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t// Initialization not complete, skip\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tclients = append(clients, con)\n\t}\n\treturn clients\n}\n\n// SortedClients returns all currently connected clients in an ordered manner.\n// Sorting order priority is as follows: ClusterID, Namespace, ID.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) SortedClients() []*Connection {\n\tclients := s.Clients()\n\tsort.Slice(clients, func(i, j int) bool {\n\t\tif clients[i].proxy.GetClusterID().String() < clients[j].proxy.GetClusterID().String() {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t\tif clients[i].proxy.GetNamespace() < clients[j].proxy.GetNamespace() {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn clients[i].proxy.GetID() < clients[j].proxy.GetID()\n\t})\n\treturn clients\n}\n\n// AllClients returns all connected clients, per Clients, but additionally includes uninitialized connections\n// Warning: callers must take care not to rely on the con.proxy field being set\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) AllClients() []*Connection {\n\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\n\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\n\treturn maps.Values(s.adsClients)\n}\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) WaitForRequestLimit(ctx context.Context) error {\n\tif s.RequestRateLimit.Limit() == 0 {\n\t\t// Allow opt out when rate limiting is set to 0qps\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\t// Give a bit of time for queue to clear out, but if not fail fast. Client will connect to another\n\t// instance in best case, or retry with backoff.\n\twait, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, time.Second)\n\tdefer cancel()\n\treturn s.RequestRateLimit.Wait(wait)\n}\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) NextVersion() string {\n\treturn time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339) + \"/\" + strconv.FormatUint(s.pushVersion.Inc(), 10)\n}\n", "numLines": 588, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 588}}

    98→// Event represents a config or registry event that results in a push.
    99→type Event struct {
   100→	// pushRequest PushRequest to use for the push.

   107→func newConnection(peerAddr string, stream DiscoveryStream) *Connection {
   108→	return &Connection{
   109→		Connection: xds.NewConnection(peerAddr, stream),
   110→	}
   111→}
   112→
   113→func (conn *Connection) Initialize(node *core.Node) error {
   114→	return conn.s.initConnection(node, conn, conn.ids)
   115→}

   117→func (conn *Connection) Close() {
   118→	conn.s.closeConnection(conn)
   119→}

   121→func (conn *Connection) Watcher() xds.Watcher {
   122→	return conn.proxy
   123→}

   125→func (conn *Connection) Process(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) error {
   126→	return conn.s.processRequest(req, conn)
   127→}
   128→
   129→func (conn *Connection) Push(ev any) error {
   130→	pushEv := ev.(*Event)
   131→	err := conn.s.pushConnection(conn, pushEv)
   132→	pushEv.done()
   133→	return err
   134→}
   135→
   136→// processRequest handles one discovery request. This is currently called from the 'main' thread, which also
   137→// handles 'push' requests and close - the code will eventually call the 'push' code, and it needs more mutex
   138→// protection. Original code avoided the mutexes by doing both 'push' and 'process requests' in same thread.
   139→func (s *DiscoveryServer) processRequest(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest, con *Connection) error {
   140→	stype := v3.GetShortType(req.TypeUrl)
   141→	log.Debugf("ADS:%s: REQ %s resources:%d nonce:%s version:%s ", stype,
   142→		con.ID(), len(req.ResourceNames), req.ResponseNonce, req.VersionInfo)
   143→	if req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {
   144→		s.handleWorkloadHealthcheck(con.proxy, req)
   145→		return nil
   146→	}
   147→
   148→	// For now, don't let xDS piggyback debug requests start watchers.
   149→	if strings.HasPrefix(req.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {
   150→		return s.pushXds(con,
   151→			&model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: req.TypeUrl, ResourceNames: sets.New(req.ResourceNames...)},
   152→			&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Push: con.proxy.LastPushContext, Forced: true})
   153→	}
   154→
   155→	shouldRespond, delta := xds.ShouldRespond(con.proxy, con.ID(), req)
   156→	if !shouldRespond {
   157→		return nil
   158→	}
   159→
   160→	request := &model.PushRequest{
   161→		Full:   true,
   162→		Push:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,
   163→		Reason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyRequest),
   164→
   165→		// The usage of LastPushTime (rather than time.Now()), is critical here for correctness; This time
   166→		// is used by the XDS cache to determine if a entry is stale. If we use Now() with an old push context,
   167→		// we may end up overriding active cache entries with stale ones.
   168→		Start:  con.proxy.LastPushTime,
   169→		Delta:  delta,
   170→		Forced: true,
   171→	}
   172→
   173→	// SidecarScope for the proxy may not have been updated based on this pushContext.
   174→	// It can happen when `processRequest` comes after push context has been updated(s.initPushContext),
   175→	// but proxy's SidecarScope has been updated(s.computeProxyState -> SetSidecarScope) due to optimizations that skip sidecar scope
   176→	// computation.
   177→	if con.proxy.SidecarScope != nil && con.proxy.SidecarScope.Version != request.Push.PushVersion {
   178→		s.computeProxyState(con.proxy, request)
   179→	}
   180→	return s.pushXds(con, con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(req.TypeUrl), request)
   181→}
   182→
   183→// StreamAggregatedResources implements the ADS interface.
   184→func (s *DiscoveryServer) StreamAggregatedResources(stream DiscoveryStream) error {
   185→	return s.Stream(stream)
   186→}
   187→
   188→func (s *DiscoveryServer) Stream(stream DiscoveryStream) error {
   189→	if knativeEnv != "" && firstRequest.Load() {
   190→		// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the "loading" request. During
   191→		// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.
   192→		// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a
   193→		// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.
   194→		firstRequest.Store(false)
   195→		return status.Error(codes.Unavailable, "server warmup not complete; try again")
   196→	}
   197→	// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.
   198→	// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build
   199→	// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would
   200→	// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing
   201→	// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding
   202→	// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy
   203→	// ip tables update latencies.
   204→	// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.
   205→	if !s.IsServerReady() {
   206→		return status.Error(codes.Unavailable, "server is not ready to serve discovery information")
   207→	}
   208→
   209→	ctx := stream.Context()
   210→	peerAddr := "0.0.0.0"
   211→	if peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {
   212→		peerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()
   213→	}
   214→
   215→	if err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {
   216→		log.Warnf("ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v", peerAddr, err)
   217→		return status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, "request rate limit exceeded: %v", err)
   218→	}
   219→
   220→	ids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)
   221→	if err != nil {
   222→		return status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())
   223→	}
   224→	if ids != nil {
   225→		log.Debugf("Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v", peerAddr, ids)
   226→	} else {
   227→		log.Debugf("Unauthenticated XDS: %s", peerAddr)
   228→	}
   229→
   230→	// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.
   231→	s.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)
   232→	con := newConnection(peerAddr, stream)
   233→	con.ids = ids
   234→	con.s = s
   235→	return xds.Stream(con)
   236→}
   237→
   238→// update the node associated with the connection, after receiving a packet from envoy, also adds the connection
   239→// to the tracking map.
   240→func (s *DiscoveryServer) initConnection(node *core.Node, con *Connection, identities []string) error {
   241→	// Setup the initial proxy metadata
   242→	proxy, err := s.initProxyMetadata(node)
   243→	if err != nil {
   244→		return err
   245→	}
   246→	// Check if proxy cluster has an alias configured, if yes use that as cluster ID for this proxy.
   247→	if alias, exists := s.ClusterAliases[proxy.Metadata.ClusterID]; exists {
   248→		proxy.Metadata.ClusterID = alias
   249→	}
   250→	// To ensure push context is monotonically increasing, setup LastPushContext before we addCon. This
   251→	// way only new push contexts will be registered for this proxy.
   252→	proxy.LastPushContext = s.globalPushContext()
   253→	// First request so initialize connection id and start tracking it.
   254→	con.SetID(connectionID(proxy.ID))
   255→	con.node = node
   256→	con.proxy = proxy
   257→	if proxy.IsZTunnel() && !features.EnableAmbient {
   258→		return fmt.Errorf("ztunnel requires PILOT_ENABLE_AMBIENT=true")
   259→	}
   260→
   261→	// Authorize xds clients
   262→	if err := s.authorize(con, identities); err != nil {
   263→		return err
   264→	}
   265→
   266→	// Register the connection. this allows pushes to be triggered for the proxy. Note: the timing of
   267→	// this and initializeProxy important. While registering for pushes *after* initialization is complete seems like
   268→	// a better choice, it introduces a race condition; If we complete initialization of a new push
   269→	// context between initializeProxy and addCon, we would not get any pushes triggered for the new
   270→	// push context, leading the proxy to have a stale state until the next full push.
   271→	s.addCon(con.ID(), con)
   272→	// Register that initialization is complete. This triggers to calls that it is safe to access the
   273→	// proxy
   274→	defer con.MarkInitialized()
   275→
   276→	// Complete full initialization of the proxy
   277→	if err := s.initializeProxy(con); err != nil {
   278→		s.closeConnection(con)
   279→		return err
   280→	}
   281→	return nil
   282→}
   283→
   284→func (s *DiscoveryServer) closeConnection(con *Connection) {
   285→	if con.ID() == "" {
   286→		return
   287→	}
   288→	s.removeCon(con.ID())
   289→	s.WorkloadEntryController.OnDisconnect(con)
   290→}
   291→
   292→func connectionID(node string) string {
   293→	id := atomic.AddInt64(&connectionNumber, 1)
   294→	return node + "-" + strconv.FormatInt(id, 10)
   295→}
   296→
   297→// initProxyMetadata initializes just the basic metadata of a proxy. This is decoupled from
   298→// initProxyState such that we can perform authorization before attempting expensive computations to
   299→// fully initialize the proxy.
   300→func (s *DiscoveryServer) initProxyMetadata(node *core.Node) (*model.Proxy, error) {
   301→	meta, err := model.ParseMetadata(node.Metadata)
   302→	if err != nil {
   303→		return nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()
   304→	}
   305→	proxy, err := model.ParseServiceNodeWithMetadata(node.Id, meta)
   306→	if err != nil {
   307→		return nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()
   308→	}
   309→	// Update the config namespace associated with this proxy
   310→	proxy.ConfigNamespace = model.GetProxyConfigNamespace(proxy)
   311→	proxy.XdsNode = node
   312→	return proxy, nil
   313→}
   314→
   315→// setTopologyLabels sets locality, cluster, network label
   316→// must be called after `SetWorkloadLabels` and `SetServiceTargets`.
   317→func setTopologyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) {
   318→	// This is a bit un-intuitive, but pull the locality from Labels first. The service registries have the best access to
   319→	// locality information, as they can read from various sources (Node on Kubernetes, for example). They will take this
   320→	// information and add it to the labels. So while the proxy may not originally have these labels,
   321→	// it will by the time we get here (as a result of calling this after SetWorkloadLabels).
   322→	proxy.Locality = localityFromProxyLabels(proxy)
   323→	if proxy.Locality == nil {
   324→		// If there is no locality in the registry then use the one sent as part of the discovery request.
   325→		// This is not preferable as only the connected Pilot is aware of this proxies location, but it
   326→		// can still help provide some client-side Envoy context when load balancing based on location.
   327→		proxy.Locality = &core.Locality{
   328→			Region:  proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetRegion(),
   329→			Zone:    proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetZone(),
   330→			SubZone: proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetSubZone(),
   331→		}
   332→	}
   333→	// add topology labels to proxy labels
   334→	proxy.Labels = labelutil.AugmentLabels(
   335→		proxy.Labels,
   336→		proxy.Metadata.ClusterID,
   337→		util.LocalityToString(proxy.Locality),
   338→		proxy.GetNodeName(),
   339→		proxy.Metadata.Network,
   340→	)
   341→}
   342→
   343→func localityFromProxyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) *core.Locality {
   344→	region, f1 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyRegion]
   345→	zone, f2 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyZone]
   346→	subzone, f3 := proxy.Labels[label.TopologySubzone.Name]
   347→	if !f1 && !f2 && !f3 {
   348→		// If no labels set, we didn't find the locality from the service registry. We do support a (mostly undocumented/internal)
   349→		// label to override the locality, so respect that here as well.
   350→		localityLabel := pm.GetLocalityLabel(proxy.Labels)
   351→		if localityLabel != "" {
   352→			return util.ConvertLocality(localityLabel)
   353→		}
   354→		return nil
   355→	}
   356→	return &core.Locality{
   357→		Region:  region,
   358→		Zone:    zone,
   359→		SubZone: subzone,
   360→	}
   361→}
   362→
   363→// initializeProxy completes the initialization of a proxy. It is expected to be called only after
   364→// initProxyMetadata.
   365→func (s *DiscoveryServer) initializeProxy(con *Connection) error {
   366→	proxy := con.proxy
   367→	// this should be done before we look for service instances, but after we load metadata
   368→	// TODO fix check in kubecontroller treat echo VMs like there isn't a pod
   369→	if err := s.WorkloadEntryController.OnConnect(con); err != nil {
   370→		return err
   371→	}
   372→	s.computeProxyState(proxy, nil)
   373→	// Discover supported IP Versions of proxy so that appropriate config can be delivered.
   374→	proxy.DiscoverIPMode()
   375→
   376→	proxy.WatchedResources = map[string]*model.WatchedResource{}
   377→	// Based on node metadata and version, we can associate a different generator.
   378→	if proxy.Metadata.Generator != "" {
   379→		proxy.XdsResourceGenerator = s.Generators[proxy.Metadata.Generator]
   380→	}
   381→
   382→	return nil
   383→}
   384→
   385→func (s *DiscoveryServer) computeProxyState(proxy *model.Proxy, request *model.PushRequest) {
   386→	proxy.Lock()
   387→	defer proxy.Unlock()
   388→	var shouldResetGateway, shouldResetSidecarScope bool
   389→	// 1. If request == nil(initiation phase) or request.ConfigsUpdated == nil(global push), set proxy serviceTargets.
   390→	// 2. otherwise only set when svc update, this is for the case that a service may select the proxy
   391→	if request == nil || request.Forced ||
   392→		proxy.ShouldUpdateServiceTargets(request.ConfigsUpdated) {
   393→		proxy.SetServiceTargets(s.Env.ServiceDiscovery)
   394→		// proxy.SetGatewaysForProxy depends on the serviceTargets,
   395→		// so when we reset serviceTargets, should reset gateway as well.
   396→		shouldResetGateway = true
   397→	}
   398→
   399→	// only recompute workload labels when
   400→	// 1. stream established and proxy first time initialization
   401→	// 2. proxy update
   402→	recomputeLabels := request == nil || request.IsProxyUpdate()
   403→	if recomputeLabels {
   404→		proxy.SetWorkloadLabels(s.Env)
   405→		setTopologyLabels(proxy)
   406→	}
   407→	// Precompute the sidecar scope and merged gateways associated with this proxy.
   408→	// Saves compute cycles in networking code. Though this might be redundant sometimes, we still
   409→	// have to compute this because as part of a config change, a new Sidecar could become
   410→	// applicable to this proxy
   411→	push := proxy.LastPushContext
   412→	if request == nil {
   413→		shouldResetSidecarScope = true
   414→	} else {
   415→		push = request.Push
   416→		if request.Forced {
   417→			shouldResetSidecarScope = true
   418→		}
   419→		for conf := range request.ConfigsUpdated {
   420→			switch conf.Kind {
   421→			case kind.ServiceEntry, kind.DestinationRule, kind.VirtualService, kind.Sidecar:
   422→				shouldResetSidecarScope = true
   423→			case kind.Gateway:
   424→				shouldResetGateway = true
   425→			case kind.Ingress:
   426→				shouldResetSidecarScope = true
   427→				shouldResetGateway = true
   428→			}
   429→			if shouldResetSidecarScope && shouldResetGateway {
   430→				break
   431→			}
   432→		}
   433→	}
   434→	// compute the sidecarscope for both proxy type whenever it changes.
   435→	if shouldResetSidecarScope {
   436→		proxy.SetSidecarScope(push)
   437→	}
   438→	// only compute gateways for "router" type proxy.
   439→	if shouldResetGateway && proxy.Type == model.Router {
   440→		proxy.SetGatewaysForProxy(push)
   441→	}
   442→	proxy.LastPushContext = push
   443→	if request != nil {
   444→		proxy.LastPushTime = request.Start
   445→	}
   446→}
   447→
   448→// handleWorkloadHealthcheck processes HealthInformation type Url.
   449→func (s *DiscoveryServer) handleWorkloadHealthcheck(proxy *model.Proxy, req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) {
   450→	if features.WorkloadEntryHealthChecks {
   451→		event := autoregistration.HealthEvent{}
   452→		event.Healthy = req.ErrorDetail == nil
   453→		if !event.Healthy {
   454→			event.Message = req.ErrorDetail.Message
   455→		}
   456→		s.WorkloadEntryController.QueueWorkloadEntryHealth(proxy, event)
   457→	}
   458→}
   459→
   460→// DeltaAggregatedResources is not implemented.
   461→// Instead, Generators may send only updates/add, with Delete indicated by an empty spec.
   462→// This works if both ends follow this model. For example EDS and the API generator follow this
   463→// pattern.
   464→//
   465→// The delta protocol changes the request, adding unsubscribe/subscribe instead of sending full
   466→// list of resources. On the response it adds 'removed resources' and sends changes for everything.
   467→func (s *DiscoveryServer) DeltaAggregatedResources(stream discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesServer) error {
   468→	return s.StreamDeltas(stream)
   469→}
   470→
   471→// Compute and send the new configuration for a connection.
   472→func (s *DiscoveryServer) pushConnection(con *Connection, pushEv *Event) error {
   473→	pushRequest := pushEv.pushRequest
   474→
   475→	if pushRequest.Full {
   476→		// Update Proxy with current information.
   477→		s.computeProxyState(con.proxy, pushRequest)
   478→	}
   479→
   480→	pushRequest, needsPush := s.ProxyNeedsPush(con.proxy, pushRequest)
   481→	if !needsPush {
   482→		log.Debugf("Skipping push to %v, no updates required", con.ID())
   483→		return nil
   484→	}
   485→
   486→	// Send pushes to all generators
   487→	// Each Generator is responsible for determining if the push event requires a push
   488→	wrl := con.watchedResourcesByOrder()
   489→	for _, w := range wrl {
   490→		if err := s.pushXds(con, w, pushRequest); err != nil {
   491→			return err
   492→		}
   493→	}
   494→	proxiesConvergeDelay.Record(time.Since(pushRequest.Start).Seconds())
   495→	return nil
   496→}
   497→
   498→// PushOrder defines the order that updates will be pushed in. Any types not listed here will be pushed in random
   499→// order after the types listed here
   500→var PushOrder = []string{
   501→	v3.ClusterType,
   502→	v3.EndpointType,
   503→	v3.ListenerType,
   504→	v3.RouteType,
   505→	v3.SecretType,
   506→	v3.AddressType,
   507→	v3.WorkloadType,
   508→	v3.WorkloadAuthorizationType,
   509→}
   510→
   511→// KnownOrderedTypeUrls has typeUrls for which we know the order of push.
   512→var KnownOrderedTypeUrls = sets.New(PushOrder...)
   513→
   514→func (s *DiscoveryServer) adsClientCount() int {
   515→	s.adsClientsMutex.RLock()
   516→	defer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()
   517→	return len(s.adsClients)
   518→}
   519→
   520→func (s *DiscoveryServer) ProxyUpdate(clusterID cluster.ID, ip string) {
   521→	var connection *Connection
   522→
   523→	for _, v := range s.Clients() {
   524→		if v.proxy.Metadata.ClusterID == clusterID && v.proxy.IPAddresses[0] == ip {
   525→			connection = v
   526→			break
   527→		}
   528→	}
   529→
   530→	// It is possible that the envoy has not connected to this pilot, maybe connected to another pilot
   531→	if connection == nil {
   532→		return
   533→	}
   534→	if log.DebugEnabled() {
   535→		currentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()
   536→		if currentlyPending != 0 {
   537→			log.Debugf("Starting new push while %v were still pending", currentlyPending)
   538→		}
   539→	}
   540→
   541→	s.pushQueue.Enqueue(connection, &model.PushRequest{
   542→		Full:   true,
   543→		Push:   s.globalPushContext(),
   544→		Start:  time.Now(),
   545→		Reason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyUpdate),
   546→		Forced: true,
   547→	})
   548→}
   549→
   550→// AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, with a full push.
   551→// Mainly used in Debug interface.
   552→func AdsPushAll(s *DiscoveryServer) {
   553→	s.AdsPushAll(&model.PushRequest{
   554→		Full:   true,
   555→		Push:   s.globalPushContext(),
   556→		Reason: model.NewReasonStats(model.DebugTrigger),
   557→		Forced: true,
   558→	})
   559→}
   560→
   561→// AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, for a full config or incremental EDS.
   562→func (s *DiscoveryServer) AdsPushAll(req *model.PushRequest) {
   563→	if !req.Full {
   564→		log.Infof("XDS: Incremental Pushing ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s",
   565→			s.adsClientCount(), req.Push.PushVersion)
   566→	} else {
   567→		totalService := len(req.Push.GetAllServices())
   568→		log.Infof("XDS: Pushing Services:%d ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s",
   569→			totalService, s.adsClientCount(), req.Push.PushVersion)
   570→		monServices.Record(float64(totalService))
   571→
   572→		// Make sure the ConfigsUpdated map exists
   573→		if req.ConfigsUpdated == nil {
   574→			req.ConfigsUpdated = make(sets.Set[model.ConfigKey])
   575→		}
   576→	}
   577→
   578→	s.StartPush(req)
   579→}
   580→
   581→// Send a signal to all connections, with a push event.
   582→func (s *DiscoveryServer) StartPush(req *model.PushRequest) {
   583→	// Push config changes, iterating over connected envoys.
   584→	if log.DebugEnabled() {
   585→		currentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()
   586→		if currentlyPending != 0 {
   587→			log.Debugf("Starting new push while %v were still pending", currentlyPending)
   588→		}
   589→	}
   590→	req.Start = time.Now()
   591→	for _, p := range s.AllClients() {
   592→		s.pushQueue.Enqueue(p, req)
   593→	}
   594→}
   595→
   596→func (s *DiscoveryServer) addCon(conID string, con *Connection) {
   597→	s.adsClientsMutex.Lock()
   598→	defer s.adsClientsMutex.Unlock()
   599→	s.adsClients[conID] = con
   600→	recordXDSClients(con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion, 1)
   601→}
   602→
   603→func (s *DiscoveryServer) removeCon(conID string) {
   604→	s.adsClientsMutex.Lock()
   605→	defer s.adsClientsMutex.Unlock()
   606→
   607→	if con, exist := s.adsClients[conID]; !exist {
   608→		log.Errorf("ADS: Removing connection for non-existing node:%v.", conID)
   609→		xds.TotalXDSInternalErrors.Increment()
   610→	} else {
   611→		delete(s.adsClients, conID)
   612→		recordXDSClients(con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion, -1)
   613→	}
   614→}
   615→
   616→func (conn *Connection) Clusters() []string {
   617→	return conn.proxy.Clusters()
   618→}
   619→
   620→// watchedResourcesByOrder returns the ordered list of
   621→// watched resources for the proxy, ordered in accordance with known push order.
   622→func (conn *Connection) watchedResourcesByOrder() []*model.WatchedResource {
   623→	allWatched := conn.proxy.ShallowCloneWatchedResources()
   624→	ordered := make([]*model.WatchedResource, 0, len(allWatched))
   625→	// first add all known types, in order
   626→	for _, tp := range PushOrder {
   627→		if allWatched[tp] != nil {
   628→			ordered = append(ordered, allWatched[tp])
   629→		}
   630→	}
   631→	// Then add any undeclared types
   632→	for tp, res := range allWatched {
   633→		if !KnownOrderedTypeUrls.Contains(tp) {
   634→			ordered = append(ordered, res)
   635→		}
   636→	}
   637→	return ordered
   638→}

implied.\n// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n// limitations under the License.\n\npackage xds\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strconv\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"sync/atomic\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\tcore \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/config/core/v3\"\n\tdiscovery \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\"\n\tuatomic \"go.uber.org/atomic\"\n\t\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\"\n\t\"google.golang.org/grpc/peer\"\n\t\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\"\n\n\t\"istio.io/api/label\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/autoregistration\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/networking/util\"\n\tlabelutil \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/serviceregistry/util/label\"\n\tv3 \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/cluster\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/kind\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/env\"\n\tpm \"istio.io/istio/pkg/model\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/xds\"\n)\n\nvar (\n\tlog = xds.Log\n\n\t// Tracks connections, increment on each new connection.\n\tconnectionNumber = int64(0)\n)\n\n// Used only when running in KNative, to handle the load balancing behavior.\nvar firstRequest = uatomic.NewBool(true)\n\nvar knativeEnv = env.Register(\"K_REVISION\", \"\",\n\t\"KNative revision, set if running in knative\").Get()\n\n// DiscoveryStream is a server interface for XDS.\ntype DiscoveryStream = xds.DiscoveryStream\n\n// DeltaDiscoveryStream is a server interface for Delta XDS.\ntype DeltaDiscoveryStream = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesServer\n\n// DiscoveryClient is a client interface for XDS.\ntype DiscoveryClient = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_StreamAggregatedResourcesClient\n\n// DeltaDiscoveryClient is a client interface for Delta XDS.\ntype DeltaDiscoveryClient = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesClient\n\ntype Connection struct {\n\txds.Connection\n\n\t// Original node metadata, to avoid unmarshal/marshal.\n\t// This is included in internal events.\n\tnode *core.Node\n\n\t// proxy is the client to which this connection is established.\n\tproxy *model.Proxy\n\n\t// deltaStream is used for Delta XDS. Only one of deltaStream or stream will be set\n\tdeltaStream DeltaDiscoveryStream\n\n\tdeltaReqChan chan *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest\n\n\ts   *DiscoveryServer\n\tids []string\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) XdsConnection() *xds.Connection {\n\treturn &conn.Connection\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Proxy() *model.Proxy {\n\treturn conn.proxy\n}\n\n// Event represents a config or registry event that results in a push.\ntype Event struct {\n\t// pushRequest PushRequest to use for the push.\n\tpushRequest *model.PushRequest\n\n\t// function to call once a push is finished. This must be called or future changes may be blocked.\n\tdone func()\n}\n\nfunc newConnection(peerAddr string, stream DiscoveryStream) *Connection {\n\treturn &Connection{\n\t\tConnection: xds.NewConnection(peerAddr, stream),\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Initialize(node *core.Node) error {\n\treturn conn.s.initConnection(node, conn, conn.ids)\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Close() {\n\tconn.s.closeConnection(conn)\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Watcher() xds.Watcher {\n\treturn conn.proxy\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Process(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) error {\n\treturn conn.s.processRequest(req, conn)\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Push(ev any) error {\n\tpushEv := ev.(*Event)\n\terr := conn.s.pushConnection(conn, pushEv)\n\tpushEv.done()\n\treturn err\n}\n\n// processRequest handles one discovery request. This is currently called from the 'main' thread, which also\n// handles 'push' requests and close - the code will eventually call the 'push' code, and it needs more mutex\n// protection. Original code avoided the mutexes by doing both 'push' and 'process requests' in same thread.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) processRequest(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest, con *Connection) error {\n\tstype := v3.GetShortType(req.TypeUrl)\n\tlog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: REQ %s resources:%d nonce:%s version:%s \", stype,\n\t\tcon.ID(), len(req.ResourceNames), req.ResponseNonce, req.VersionInfo)\n\tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\n\t\ts.handleWorkloadHealthcheck(con.proxy, req)\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// For now, don't let xDS piggyback debug requests start watchers.\n\tif strings.HasPrefix(req.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\n\t\treturn s.pushXds(con,\n\t\t\t&model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: req.TypeUrl, ResourceNames: sets.New(req.ResourceNames...)},\n\t\t\t&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Push: con.proxy.LastPushContext, Forced: true})\n\t}\n\n\tshouldRespond, delta := xds.ShouldRespond(con.proxy, con.ID(), req)\n\tif !shouldRespond {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\trequest := &model.PushRequest{\n\t\tFull:   true,\n\t\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\n\t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyRequest),\n\n\t\t// The usage of LastPushTime (rather than time.Now()), is critical here for correctness; This time\n\t\t// is used by the XDS cache to determine if a entry is stale. If we use Now() with an old push context,\n\t\t// we may end up overriding active cache entries with stale ones.\n\t\tStart:  con.proxy.LastPushTime,\n\t\tDelta:  delta,\n\t\tForced: true,\n\t}\n\n\t// SidecarScope for the proxy may not have been updated based on this pushContext.\n\t// It can happen when `processRequest` comes after push context has been updated(s.initPushContext),\n\t// but proxy's SidecarScope has been updated(s.computeProxyState -> SetSidecarScope) due to optimizations that skip sidecar scope\n\t// computation.\n\tif con.proxy.SidecarScope != nil && con.proxy.SidecarScope.Version != request.Push.PushVersion {\n\t\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, request)\n\t}\n\treturn s.pushXds(con, con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(req.TypeUrl), request)\n}\n\n// StreamAggregatedResources implements the ADS interface.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) StreamAggregatedResources(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\n\treturn s.Stream(stream)\n}\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) Stream(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\n\tif knativeEnv != \"\" && firstRequest.Load() {\n\t\t// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the \"loading\" request. During\n\t\t// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.\n\t\t// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a\n\t\t// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.\n\t\tfirstRequest.Store(false)\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server warmup not complete; try again\")\n\t}\n\t// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.\n\t// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build\n\t// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would\n\t// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing\n\t// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding\n\t// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy\n\t// ip tables update latencies.\n\t// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.\n\tif !s.IsServerReady() {\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server is not ready to serve discovery information\")\n\t}\n\n\tctx := stream.Context()\n\tpeerAddr := \"0.0.0.0\"\n\tif peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {\n\t\tpeerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()\n\t}\n\n\tif err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Warnf(\"ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v\", peerAddr, err)\n\t\treturn status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, \"request rate limit exceeded: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())\n\t}\n\tif ids != nil {\n\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v\", peerAddr, ids)\n\t} else {\n\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Unauthenticated XDS: %s\", peerAddr)\n\t}\n\n\t// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.\n\ts.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)\n\tcon := newConnection(peerAddr, stream)\n\tcon.ids = ids\n\tcon.s = s\n\treturn xds.Stream(con)\n}\n\n// update the node associated with the connection, after receiving a packet from envoy, also adds the connection\n// to the tracking map.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) initConnection(node *core.Node, con *Connection, identities []string) error {\n\t// Setup the initial proxy metadata\n\tproxy, err := s.initProxyMetadata(node)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\t// Check if proxy cluster has an alias configured, if yes use that as cluster ID for this proxy.\n\tif alias, exists := s.ClusterAliases[proxy.Metadata.ClusterID]; exists {\n\t\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID = alias\n\t}\n\t// To ensure push context is monotonically increasing, setup LastPushContext before we addCon. This\n\t// way only new push contexts will be registered for this proxy.\n\tproxy.LastPushContext = s.globalPushContext()\n\t// First request so initialize connection id and start tracking it.\n\tcon.SetID(connectionID(proxy.ID))\n\tcon.node = node\n\tcon.proxy = proxy\n\tif proxy.IsZTunnel() && !features.EnableAmbient {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"ztunnel requires PILOT_ENABLE_AMBIENT=true\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Authorize xds clients\n\tif err := s.authorize(con, identities); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\t// Register the connection. this allows pushes to be triggered for the proxy. Note: the timing of\n\t// this and initializeProxy important. While registering for pushes *after* initialization is complete seems like\n\t// a better choice, it introduces a race condition; If we complete initialization of a new push\n\t// context between initializeProxy and addCon, we would not get any pushes triggered for the new\n\t// push context, leading the proxy to have a stale state until the next full push.\n\ts.addCon(con.ID(), con)\n\t// Register that initialization is complete. This triggers to calls that it is safe to access the\n\t// proxy\n\tdefer con.MarkInitialized()\n\n\t// Complete full initialization of the proxy\n\tif err := s.initializeProxy(con); err != nil {\n\t\ts.closeConnection(con)\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) closeConnection(con *Connection) {\n\tif con.ID() == \"\" {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\ts.removeCon(con.ID())\n\ts.WorkloadEntryController.OnDisconnect(con)\n}\n\nfunc connectionID(node string) string {\n\tid := atomic.AddInt64(&connectionNumber, 1)\n\treturn node + \"-\" + strconv.FormatInt(id, 10)\n}\n\n// initProxyMetadata initializes just the basic metadata of a proxy. This is decoupled from\n// initProxyState such that we can perform authorization before attempting expensive computations to\n// fully initialize the proxy.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) initProxyMetadata(node *core.Node) (*model.Proxy, error) {\n\tmeta, err := model.ParseMetadata(node.Metadata)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\n\t}\n\tproxy, err := model.ParseServiceNodeWithMetadata(node.Id, meta)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\n\t}\n\t// Update the config namespace associated with this proxy\n\tproxy.ConfigNamespace = model.GetProxyConfigNamespace(proxy)\n\tproxy.XdsNode = node\n\treturn proxy, nil\n}\n\n// setTopologyLabels sets locality, cluster, network label\n// must be called after `SetWorkloadLabels` and `SetServiceTargets`.\nfunc setTopologyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) {\n\t// This is a bit un-intuitive, but pull the locality from Labels first. The service registries have the best access to\n\t// locality information, as they can read from various sources (Node on Kubernetes, for example). They will take this\n\t// information and add it to the labels. So while the proxy may not originally have these labels,\n\t// it will by the time we get here (as a result of calling this after SetWorkloadLabels).\n\tproxy.Locality = localityFromProxyLabels(proxy)\n\tif proxy.Locality == nil {\n\t\t// If there is no locality in the registry then use the one sent as part of the discovery request.\n\t\t// This is not preferable as only the connected Pilot is aware of this proxies location, but it\n\t\t// can still help provide some client-side Envoy context when load balancing based on location.\n\t\tproxy.Locality = &core.Locality{\n\t\t\tRegion:  proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetRegion(),\n\t\t\tZone:    proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetZone(),\n\t\t\tSubZone: proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetSubZone(),\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// add topology labels to proxy labels\n\tproxy.Labels = labelutil.AugmentLabels(\n\t\tproxy.Labels,\n\t\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID,\n\t\tutil.LocalityToString(proxy.Locality),\n\t\tproxy.GetNodeName(),\n\t\tproxy.Metadata.Network,\n\t)\n}\n\nfunc localityFromProxyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) *core.Locality {\n\tregion, f1 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyRegion]\n\tzone, f2 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyZone]\n\tsubzone, f3 := proxy.Labels[label.TopologySubzone.Name]\n\tif !f1 && !f2 && !f3 {\n\t\t// If no labels set, we didn't find the locality from the service registry. We do support a (mostly undocumented/internal)\n\t\t// label to override the locality, so respect that here as well.\n\t\tlocalityLabel := pm.GetLocalityLabel(proxy.Labels)\n\t\tif localityLabel != \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn util.ConvertLocality(localityLabel)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn &core.Locality{\n\t\tRegion:  region,\n\t\tZone:    zone,\n\t\tSubZone: subzone,\n\t}\n}\n\n// initializeProxy completes the initialization of a proxy. It is expected to be called only after\n// initProxyMetadata.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) initializeProxy(con *Connection) error {\n\tproxy := con.proxy\n\t// this should be done before we look for service instances, but after we load metadata\n\t// TODO fix check in kubecontroller treat echo VMs like there isn't a pod\n\tif err := s.WorkloadEntryController.OnConnect(con); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\ts.computeProxyState(proxy, nil)\n\t// Discover supported IP Versions of proxy so that appropriate config can be delivered.\n\tproxy.DiscoverIPMode()\n\n\tproxy.WatchedResources = map[string]*model.WatchedResource{}\n\t// Based on node metadata and version, we can associate a different generator.\n\tif proxy.Metadata.Generator != \"\" {\n\t\tproxy.XdsResourceGenerator = s.Generators[proxy.Metadata.Generator]\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) computeProxyState(proxy *model.Proxy, request *model.PushRequest) {\n\tproxy.Lock()\n\tdefer proxy.Unlock()\n\tvar shouldResetGateway, shouldResetSidecarScope bool\n\t// 1. If request == nil(initiation phase) or request.ConfigsUpdated == nil(global push), set proxy serviceTargets.\n\t// 2. otherwise only set when svc update, this is for the case that a service may select the proxy\n\tif request == nil || request.Forced ||\n\t\tproxy.ShouldUpdateServiceTargets(request.ConfigsUpdated) {\n\t\tproxy.SetServiceTargets(s.Env.ServiceDiscovery)\n\t\t// proxy.SetGatewaysForProxy depends on the serviceTargets,\n\t\t// so when we reset serviceTargets, should reset gateway as well.\n\t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n\t}\n\n\t// only recompute workload labels when\n\t// 1. stream established and proxy first time initialization\n\t// 2. proxy update\n\trecomputeLabels := request == nil || request.IsProxyUpdate()\n\tif recomputeLabels {\n\t\tproxy.SetWorkloadLabels(s.Env)\n\t\tsetTopologyLabels(proxy)\n\t}\n\t// Precompute the sidecar scope and merged gateways associated with this proxy.\n\t// Saves compute cycles in networking code. Though this might be redundant sometimes, we still\n\t// have to compute this because as part of a config change, a new Sidecar could become\n\t// applicable to this proxy\n\tpush := proxy.LastPushContext\n\tif request == nil {\n\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t} else {\n\t\tpush = request.Push\n\t\tif request.Forced {\n\t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor conf := range request.ConfigsUpdated {\n\t\t\tswitch conf.Kind {\n\t\t\tcase kind.ServiceEntry, kind.DestinationRule, kind.VirtualService, kind.Sidecar:\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t\t\tcase kind.Gateway:\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n\t\t\tcase kind.Ingress:\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif shouldResetSidecarScope && shouldResetGateway {\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// compute the sidecarscope for both proxy type whenever it changes.\n\tif shouldResetSidecarScope {\n\t\tproxy.SetSidecarScope(push)\n\t}\n\t// only compute gateways for \"router\" type proxy.\n\tif shouldResetGateway && proxy.Type == model.Router {\n\t\tproxy.SetGatewaysForProxy(push)\n\t}\n\tproxy.LastPushContext = push\n\tif request != nil {\n\t\tproxy.LastPushTime = request.Start\n\t}\n}\n\n// handleWorkloadHealthcheck processes HealthInformation type Url.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) handleWorkloadHealthcheck(proxy *model.Proxy, req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) {\n\tif features.WorkloadEntryHealthChecks {\n\t\tevent := autoregistration.HealthEvent{}\n\t\tevent.Healthy = req.ErrorDetail == nil\n\t\tif !event.Healthy {\n\t\t\tevent.Message = req.ErrorDetail.Message\n\t\t}\n\t\ts.WorkloadEntryController.QueueWorkloadEntryHealth(proxy, event)\n\t}\n}\n\n// DeltaAggregatedResources is not implemented.\n// Instead, Generators may send only updates/add, with Delete indicated by an empty spec.\n// This works if both ends follow this model. For example EDS and the API generator follow this\n// pattern.\n//\n// The delta protocol changes the request, adding unsubscribe/subscribe instead of sending full\n// list of resources. On the response it adds 'removed resources' and sends changes for everything.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) DeltaAggregatedResources(stream discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesServer) error {\n\treturn s.StreamDeltas(stream)\n}\n\n// Compute and send the new configuration for a connection.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) pushConnection(con *Connection, pushEv *Event) error {\n\tpushRequest := pushEv.pushRequest\n\n\tif pushRequest.Full {\n\t\t// Update Proxy with current information.\n\t\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, pushRequest)\n\t}\n\n\tpushRequest, needsPush := s.ProxyNeedsPush(con.proxy, pushRequest)\n\tif !needsPush {\n\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Skipping push to %v, no updates required\", con.ID())\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Send pushes to all generators\n\t// Each Generator is responsible for determining if the push event requires a push\n\twrl := con.watchedResourcesByOrder()\n\tfor _, w := range wrl {\n\t\tif err := s.pushXds(con, w, pushRequest); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tproxiesConvergeDelay.Record(time.Since(pushRequest.Start).Seconds())\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// PushOrder defines the order that updates will be pushed in. Any types not listed here will be pushed in random\n// order after the types listed here\nvar PushOrder = []string{\n\tv3.ClusterType,\n\tv3.EndpointType,\n\tv3.ListenerType,\n\tv3.RouteType,\n\tv3.SecretType,\n\tv3.AddressType,\n\tv3.WorkloadType,\n\tv3.WorkloadAuthorizationType,\n}\n\n// KnownOrderedTypeUrls has typeUrls for which we know the order of push.\nvar KnownOrderedTypeUrls = sets.New(PushOrder...)\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) adsClientCount() int {\n\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\n\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\n\treturn len(s.adsClients)\n}\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) ProxyUpdate(clusterID cluster.ID, ip string) {\n\tvar connection *Connection\n\n\tfor _, v := range s.Clients() {\n\t\tif v.proxy.Metadata.ClusterID == clusterID && v.proxy.IPAddresses[0] == ip {\n\t\t\tconnection = v\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// It is possible that the envoy has not connected to this pilot, maybe connected to another pilot\n\tif connection == nil {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\n\t\tcurrentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()\n\t\tif currentlyPending != 0 {\n\t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Starting new push while %v were still pending\", currentlyPending)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\ts.pushQueue.Enqueue(connection, &model.PushRequest{\n\t\tFull:   true,\n\t\tPush:   s.globalPushContext(),\n\t\tStart:  time.Now(),\n\t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyUpdate),\n\t\tForced: true,\n\t})\n}\n\n// AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, with a full push.\n// Mainly used in Debug interface.\nfunc AdsPushAll(s *DiscoveryServer) {\n\ts.AdsPushAll(&model.PushRequest{\n\t\tFull:   true,\n\t\tPush:   s.globalPushContext(),\n\t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.DebugTrigger),\n\t\tForced: true,\n\t})\n}\n\n// AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, for a full config or incremental EDS.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) AdsPushAll(req *model.PushRequest) {\n\tif !req.Full {\n\t\tlog.Infof(\"XDS: Incremental Pushing ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s\",\n\t\t\ts.adsClientCount(), req.Push.PushVersion)\n\t} else {\n\t\ttotalService := len(req.Push.GetAllServices())\n\t\tlog.Infof(\"XDS: Pushing Services:%d ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s\",\n\t\t\ttotalService, s.adsClientCount(), req.Push.PushVersion)\n\t\tmonServices.Record(float64(totalService))\n\n\t\t// Make sure the ConfigsUpdated map exists\n\t\tif req.ConfigsUpdated == nil {\n\t\t\treq.ConfigsUpdated = make(sets.Set[model.ConfigKey])\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\ts.StartPush(req)\n}\n\n// Send a signal to all connections, with a push event.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) StartPush(req *model.PushRequest) {\n\t// Push config changes, iterating over connected envoys.\n\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\n\t\tcurrentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()\n\t\tif currentlyPending != 0 {\n\t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Starting new push while %v were still pending\", currentlyPending)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treq.Start = time.Now()\n\tfor _, p := range s.AllClients() {\n\t\ts.pushQueue.Enqueue(p, req)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) addCon(conID string, con *Connection) {\n\ts.adsClientsMutex.Lock()\n\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.Unlock()\n\ts.adsClients[conID] = con\n\trecordXDSClients(con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion, 1)\n}\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) removeCon(conID string) {\n\ts.adsClientsMutex.Lock()\n\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.Unlock()\n\n\tif con, exist := s.adsClients[conID]; !exist {\n\t\tlog.Errorf(\"ADS: Removing connection for non-existing node:%v.\", conID)\n\t\txds.TotalXDSInternalErrors.Increment()\n\t} else {\n\t\tdelete(s.adsClients, conID)\n\t\trecordXDSClients(con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion, -1)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Clusters() []string {\n\treturn conn.proxy.Clusters()\n}\n\n// watchedResourcesByOrder returns the ordered list of\n// watched resources for the proxy, ordered in accordance with known push order.\nfunc (conn *Connection) watchedResourcesByOrder() []*model.WatchedResource {\n\tallWatched := conn.proxy.ShallowCloneWatchedResources()\n\tordered := make([]*model.WatchedResource, 0, len(allWatched))\n\t// first add all known types, in order\n\tfor _, tp := range PushOrder {\n\t\tif allWatched[tp] != nil {\n\t\t\tordered = append(ordered, allWatched[tp])\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// Then add any undeclared types\n\tfor tp, res := range allWatched {\n\t\tif !KnownOrderedTypeUrls.Contains(tp) {\n\t\t\tordered = append(ordered, res)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn ordered\n}\n", "numLines": 639, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 639}}

    56→// Metrics is an interface for capturing metrics on a per-node basis.
    57→type Metrics interface {
    58→	// AddMetric will add an case to the metric for the given node.
    59→	AddMetric(metric monitoring.Metric, key string, proxyID, msg string)

    64→// serviceIndex is an index of all services by various fields for easy access during push.
    65→type serviceIndex struct {
    66→	// privateByNamespace are services that can reachable within the same namespace, with exportTo "."
    67→	privateByNamespace map[string][]*Service
    68→	// public are services reachable within the mesh with exportTo "*"
    69→	public []*Service
    70→	// exportedToNamespace are services that were made visible to this namespace
    71→	// by an exportTo explicitly specifying this namespace.
    72→	exportedToNamespace map[string][]*Service
    73→
    74→	// HostnameAndNamespace has all services, indexed by hostname then namespace.
    75→	HostnameAndNamespace map[host.Name]map[string]*Service `json:"-"`
    76→
    77→	// instancesByPort contains a map of service key and instances by port. It is stored here
    78→	// to avoid recomputations during push. This caches instanceByPort calls with empty labels.
    79→	// Call InstancesByPort directly when instances need to be filtered by actual labels.
    80→	instancesByPort map[string]map[int][]*IstioEndpoint
    81→}
    82→
    83→func newServiceIndex() serviceIndex {
    84→	return serviceIndex{
    85→		public:               []*Service{},
    86→		privateByNamespace:   map[string][]*Service{},
    87→		exportedToNamespace:  map[string][]*Service{},
    88→		HostnameAndNamespace: map[host.Name]map[string]*Service{},
    89→		instancesByPort:      map[string]map[int][]*IstioEndpoint{},
    90→	}
    91→}

    96→	virtualService  sets.Set[visibility.Instance]

   100→// virtualServiceIndex is the index of virtual services by various fields.
   101→type virtualServiceIndex struct {
   102→	exportedToNamespaceByGateway map[types.NamespacedName][]config.Config
   103→	// this contains all the virtual services with exportTo "." and current namespace. The keys are namespace,gateway.
   104→	privateByNamespaceAndGateway map[types.NamespacedName][]config.Config
   105→	// This contains all virtual services whose exportTo is "*", keyed by gateway
   106→	publicByGateway map[string][]config.Config
   107→	// root vs namespace/name ->delegate vs virtualservice gvk/namespace/name
   108→	delegates map[ConfigKey][]ConfigKey
   109→
   110→	// This contains destination hosts of virtual services, keyed by gateway's namespace/name,
   111→	// only used when PILOT_FILTER_GATEWAY_CLUSTER_CONFIG is enabled
   112→	destinationsByGateway map[string]sets.String
   113→
   114→	// Map of VS hostname -> referenced hostnames
   115→	referencedDestinations map[string]sets.String
   116→}

   119→	out := virtualServiceIndex{

   124→		referencedDestinations:       map[string]sets.String{},
   125→	}
   126→	if features.FilterGatewayClusterConfig {

   128→	}
   129→	return out

   311→type EdsUpdateFn func(shard ShardKey, hostname string, namespace string, entry []*IstioEndpoint)
   312→
   313→// XDSUpdater is used for direct updates of the xDS model and incremental push.
   314→// Pilot uses multiple registries - for example each K8S cluster is a registry
   315→// instance. Each registry is responsible for tracking a set
   316→// of endpoints associated with mesh services, and calling the EDSUpdate on changes.
   317→// A registry may group endpoints for a service in smaller subsets - for example by
   318→// deployment, or to deal with very large number of endpoints for a service. We want
   319→// to avoid passing around large objects - like full list of endpoints for a registry,
   320→// or the full list of endpoints for a service across registries, since it limits
   321→// scalability.
   322→//
   323→// Future optimizations will include grouping the endpoints by labels, gateway or region to
   324→// reduce the time when subsetting or split-horizon is used. This design assumes pilot
   325→// tracks all endpoints in the mesh and they fit in RAM - so limit is few M endpoints.
   326→// It is possible to split the endpoint tracking in future.
   327→type XDSUpdater interface {
   328→	// EDSUpdate is called when the list of endpoints or labels in a Service is changed.
   329→	// For each cluster and hostname, the full list of active endpoints (including empty list)
   330→	// must be sent. The shard name is used as a key - current implementation is using the
   331→	// registry name.
   332→	EDSUpdate(shard ShardKey, hostname string, namespace string, entry []*IstioEndpoint)
   333→
   334→	// EDSCacheUpdate is called when the list of endpoints or labels in a Service is changed.
   335→	// For each cluster and hostname, the full list of active endpoints (including empty list)
   336→	// must be sent. The shard name is used as a key - current implementation is using the
   337→	// registry name.
   338→	// Note: the difference with `EDSUpdate` is that it only update the cache rather than requesting a push
   339→	EDSCacheUpdate(shard ShardKey, hostname string, namespace string, entry []*IstioEndpoint)
   340→
   341→	// SvcUpdate is called when a service definition is updated/deleted.
   342→	SvcUpdate(shard ShardKey, hostname string, namespace string, event Event)
   343→
   344→	// ConfigUpdate is called to notify the XDS server of config updates and request a push.
   345→	// The requests may be collapsed and throttled.
   346→	ConfigUpdate(req *PushRequest)
   347→
   348→	// ProxyUpdate is called to notify the XDS server to send a push to the specified proxy.
   349→	// The requests may be collapsed and throttled.

   355→
   356→// PushRequest defines a request to push to proxies
   357→// It is used to send updates to the config update debouncer and pass to the PushQueue.
   358→type PushRequest struct {
   359→	// Full determines whether a full push is required or not. If false, an incremental update will be sent.
   360→	// Incremental pushes:
   361→	// * Do not recompute the push context
   362→	// * Do not recompute proxy state (such as ServiceInstances)
   363→	// * Are not reported in standard metrics such as push time
   364→	// As a result, configuration updates should never be incremental. Generally, only EDS will set this, but
   365→	// in the future SDS will as well.
   366→	Full bool
   367→
   368→	// ConfigsUpdated keeps track of configs that have changed.
   369→	// This is used as an optimization to avoid unnecessary pushes to proxies that are scoped with a Sidecar.
   370→	// If this is empty, then all proxies will get an update.
   371→	// Otherwise only proxies depend on these configs will get an update.
   372→	// The kind of resources are defined in pkg/config/schemas.
   373→	ConfigsUpdated sets.Set[ConfigKey]
   374→
   375→	AddressesUpdated sets.Set[string]
   376→
   377→	// Push stores the push context to use for the update. This may initially be nil, as we will
   378→	// debounce changes before a PushContext is eventually created.
   379→	Push *PushContext
   380→
   381→	// Start represents the time a push was started. This represents the time of adding to the PushQueue.
   382→	// Note that this does not include time spent debouncing.
   383→	Start time.Time
   384→
   385→	// Reason represents the reason for requesting a push. This should only be a fixed set of values,
   386→	// to avoid unbounded cardinality in metrics. If this is not set, it may be automatically filled in later.
   387→	// There should only be multiple reasons if the push request is the result of two distinct triggers, rather than
   388→	// classifying a single trigger as having multiple reasons.
   389→	Reason ReasonStats
   390→
   391→	// Delta defines the resources that were added or removed as part of this push request.
   392→	// This is set only on requests from the client which change the set of resources they (un)subscribe from.
   393→	Delta ResourceDelta
   394→
   395→	// Forced defines that configs should be generated and pushed regardless if they have changed or not.
   396→	Forced bool
   397→}
   398→
   399→type ResourceDelta = xds.ResourceDelta
   400→
   401→type ReasonStats map[TriggerReason]int
   402→
   403→func NewReasonStats(reasons ...TriggerReason) ReasonStats {
   404→	ret := make(ReasonStats)
   405→	for _, reason := range reasons {
   406→		ret.Add(reason)
   407→	}
   408→	return ret
   409→}
   410→
   411→func (r ReasonStats) Add(reason TriggerReason) {
   412→	r[reason]++
   413→}
   414→
   415→func (r ReasonStats) Merge(other ReasonStats) {
   416→	for reason, count := range other {
   417→		r[reason] += count
   418→	}
   419→}
   420→
   421→func (r ReasonStats) CopyMerge(other ReasonStats) ReasonStats {
   422→	if len(r) == 0 {
   423→		return other
   424→	}
   425→	if len(other) == 0 {
   426→		return r
   427→	}
   428→
   429→	merged := make(ReasonStats, len(r)+len(other))
   430→	merged.Merge(r)
   431→	merged.Merge(other)
   432→
   433→	return merged
   434→}
   435→
   436→func (r ReasonStats) Count() int {
   437→	var ret int
   438→	for _, count := range r {
   439→		ret += count
   440→	}
   441→	return ret
   442→}
   443→
   444→func (r ReasonStats) Has(reason TriggerReason) bool {
   445→	return r[reason] > 0
   446→}
   447→
   448→type TriggerReason string
   449→
   450→// If adding a new reason, update xds/monitoring.go:triggerMetric
   451→const (
   452→	// EndpointUpdate describes a push triggered by an Endpoint change
   453→	EndpointUpdate TriggerReason = "endpoint"
   454→	// HeadlessEndpointUpdate describes a push triggered by an Endpoint change for headless service
   455→	HeadlessEndpointUpdate TriggerReason = "headlessendpoint"
   456→	// ConfigUpdate describes a push triggered by a config (generally and Istio CRD) change.
   457→	ConfigUpdate TriggerReason = "config"
   458→	// ServiceUpdate describes a push triggered by a Service change
   459→	ServiceUpdate TriggerReason = "service"
   460→	// ProxyUpdate describes a push triggered by a change to an individual proxy (such as label change)
   461→	ProxyUpdate TriggerReason = "proxy"
   462→	// GlobalUpdate describes a push triggered by a change to global config, such as mesh config
   463→	GlobalUpdate TriggerReason = "global"
   464→	// AmbientUpdate describes a push triggered by a change to ambient mesh config
   465→	AmbientUpdate TriggerReason = "ambient"
   466→	// UnknownTrigger describes a push triggered by an unknown reason
   467→	UnknownTrigger TriggerReason = "unknown"
   468→	// DebugTrigger describes a push triggered for debugging
   469→	DebugTrigger TriggerReason = "debug"
   470→	// SecretTrigger describes a push triggered for a Secret change
   471→	SecretTrigger TriggerReason = "secret"
   472→	// NetworksTrigger describes a push triggered for Networks change
   473→	NetworksTrigger TriggerReason = "networks"
   474→	// ProxyRequest describes a push triggered based on proxy request
   475→	ProxyRequest TriggerReason = "proxyrequest"
   476→	// DependentResource describes a push triggered based on a proxy request for a
   477→	// resource that depends on this resource (e.g. a CDS request triggers an EDS response as well)
   478→	// This is mainly used in Delta for now.
   479→	DependentResource TriggerReason = "depdendentresource"
   480→	// NamespaceUpdate describes a push triggered by a Namespace change
   481→	NamespaceUpdate TriggerReason = "namespace"
   482→	// ClusterUpdate describes a push triggered by a Cluster change
   483→	ClusterUpdate TriggerReason = "cluster"
   484→	// TagUpdate occurs when the revision's tags change, and all resources must be recalculated.
   485→	TagUpdate TriggerReason = "tag"
   486→)
   487→
   488→// Merge two update requests together
   489→// Merge behaves similarly to a list append; usage should in the form `a = a.merge(b)`.
   490→// Importantly, Merge may decide to allocate a new PushRequest object or reuse the existing one - both

    30→// SvcUpdate is a callback from service discovery when service info changes.
    31→func (s *DiscoveryServer) SvcUpdate(shard model.ShardKey, hostname string, namespace string, event model.Event) {
    32→	// When a service deleted, we should cleanup the endpoint shards and also remove keys from EndpointIndex to
    33→	// prevent memory leaks.
    34→	if event == model.EventDelete {
    35→		inboundServiceDeletes.Increment()
    36→		s.Env.EndpointIndex.DeleteServiceShard(shard, hostname, namespace, false)
    37→	} else {
    38→		inboundServiceUpdates.Increment()
    39→	}
    40→}
    41→
    42→// EDSUpdate computes destination address membership across all clusters and networks.
    43→// This is the main method implementing EDS.
    44→// It replaces InstancesByPort in model - instead of iterating over all endpoints it uses
    45→// the hostname-keyed map. And it avoids the conversion from Endpoint to ServiceEntry to envoy
    46→// on each step: instead the conversion happens once, when an endpoint is first discovered.
    47→func (s *DiscoveryServer) EDSUpdate(shard model.ShardKey, serviceName string, namespace string,
    48→	istioEndpoints []*model.IstioEndpoint,
    49→) {
    50→	inboundEDSUpdates.Increment()
    51→	// Update the endpoint shards
    52→	pushType := s.Env.EndpointIndex.UpdateServiceEndpoints(shard, serviceName, namespace, istioEndpoints, true)
    53→	if pushType == model.IncrementalPush || pushType == model.FullPush {
    54→		// Trigger a push
    55→		s.ConfigUpdate(&model.PushRequest{
    56→			Full:           pushType == model.FullPush,
    57→			ConfigsUpdated: sets.New(model.ConfigKey{Kind: kind.ServiceEntry, Name: serviceName, Namespace: namespace}),
    58→			Reason:         model.NewReasonStats(model.EndpointUpdate),
    59→		})
    60→	}
    61→}
    62→
    63→// EDSCacheUpdate computes destination address membership across all clusters and networks.
    64→// This is the main method implementing EDS.
    65→// It replaces InstancesByPort in model - instead of iterating over all endpoints it uses
    66→// the hostname-keyed map. And it avoids the conversion from Endpoint to ServiceEntry to envoy
    67→// on each step: instead the conversion happens once, when an endpoint is first discovered.
    68→//
    69→// Note: the difference with `EDSUpdate` is that it only update the cache rather than requesting a push
    70→func (s *DiscoveryServer) EDSCacheUpdate(shard model.ShardKey, serviceName string, namespace string,
    71→	istioEndpoints []*model.IstioEndpoint,
    72→) {
    73→	inboundEDSUpdates.Increment()
    74→	// Update the endpoint shards
    75→	s.Env.EndpointIndex.UpdateServiceEndpoints(shard, serviceName, namespace, istioEndpoints, false)
    76→}
    77→
    78→func (s *DiscoveryServer) RemoveShard(shardKey model.ShardKey) {
    79→	s.Env.EndpointIndex.DeleteShard(shardKey)
    80→}

And it avoids the conversion from Endpoint to ServiceEntry to envoy\n// on each step: instead the conversion happens once, when an endpoint is first discovered.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) EDSUpdate(shard model.ShardKey, serviceName string, namespace string,\n\tistioEndpoints []*model.IstioEndpoint,\n) {\n\tinboundEDSUpdates.Increment()\n\t// Update the endpoint shards\n\tpushType := s.Env.EndpointIndex.UpdateServiceEndpoints(shard, serviceName, namespace, istioEndpoints, true)\n\tif pushType == model.IncrementalPush || pushType == model.FullPush {\n\t\t// Trigger a push\n\t\ts.ConfigUpdate(&model.PushRequest{\n\t\t\tFull:           pushType == model.FullPush,\n\t\t\tConfigsUpdated: sets.New(model.ConfigKey{Kind: kind.ServiceEntry, Name: serviceName, Namespace: namespace}),\n\t\t\tReason:         model.NewReasonStats(model.EndpointUpdate),\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// EDSCacheUpdate computes destination address membership across all clusters and networks.\n// This is the main method implementing EDS.\n// It replaces InstancesByPort in model - instead of iterating over all endpoints it uses\n// the hostname-keyed map. And it avoids the conversion from Endpoint to ServiceEntry to envoy\n// on each step: instead the conversion happens once, when an endpoint is first discovered.\n//\n// Note: the difference with `EDSUpdate` is that it only update the cache rather than requesting a push\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) EDSCacheUpdate(shard model.ShardKey, serviceName string, namespace string,\n\tistioEndpoints []*model.IstioEndpoint,\n) {\n\tinboundEDSUpdates.Increment()\n\t// Update the endpoint shards\n\ts.Env.EndpointIndex.UpdateServiceEndpoints(shard, serviceName, namespace, istioEndpoints, false)\n}\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) RemoveShard(shardKey model.ShardKey) {\n\ts.Env.EndpointIndex.DeleteShard(shardKey)\n}\n\n// EdsGenerator implements the new Generate method for EDS, using the in-memory, optimized endpoint\n// storage in DiscoveryServer.\ntype EdsGenerator struct {\n\tCache         model.XdsCache\n\tEndpointIndex *model.EndpointIndex\n}\n\nvar _ model.XdsDeltaResourceGenerator = &EdsGenerator{}\n\n// Map of all configs that do not impact EDS\nvar skippedEdsConfigs = sets.New(\n\tkind.Gateway,\n\tkind.VirtualService,\n\tkind.WorkloadGroup,\n\tkind.AuthorizationPolicy,\n\tkind.RequestAuthentication,\n\tkind.Secret,\n\tkind.Telemetry,\n\tkind.WasmPlugin,", "numLines": 100, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 288}}

    15→package core
    16→
    17→import (
    18→	core "github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/config/core/v3"
    19→	listener "github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/config/listener/v3"
    20→	discovery "github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3"
    21→
    22→	meshconfig "istio.io/api/mesh/v1alpha1"
    23→	"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model"
    24→	dnsProto "istio.io/istio/pkg/dns/proto"
    25→)
    26→
    27→// ConfigGenerator represents the interfaces to be implemented by code that generates xDS responses
    28→type ConfigGenerator interface {
    29→	// BuildListeners returns the list of inbound/outbound listeners for the given proxy. This is the LDS output
    30→	// Internally, the computation will be optimized to ensure that listeners are computed only
    31→	// once and shared across multiple invocations of this function.
    32→	BuildListeners(node *model.Proxy, push *model.PushContext) []*listener.Listener
    33→
    34→	// BuildClusters returns the list of clusters for the given proxy. This is the CDS output
    35→	BuildClusters(node *model.Proxy, req *model.PushRequest) ([]*discovery.Resource, model.XdsLogDetails)
    36→
    37→	// BuildDeltaClusters returns both a list of resources that need to be pushed for a given proxy and a list of resources
    38→	// that have been deleted and should be removed from a given proxy. This is Delta CDS output.
    39→	BuildDeltaClusters(proxy *model.Proxy, updates *model.PushRequest,
    40→		watched *model.WatchedResource) ([]*discovery.Resource, []string, model.XdsLogDetails, bool)
    41→
    42→	// BuildHTTPRoutes returns the list of HTTP routes for the given proxy. This is the RDS output
    43→	BuildHTTPRoutes(node *model.Proxy, req *model.PushRequest, routeNames []string) ([]*discovery.Resource, model.XdsLogDetails)
    44→
    45→	// BuildNameTable returns list of hostnames and the associated IPs
    46→	BuildNameTable(node *model.Proxy, push *model.PushContext) *dnsProto.NameTable
    47→
    48→	// BuildExtensionConfiguration returns the list of extension configuration for the given proxy and list of names. This is the ECDS output.
    49→	BuildExtensionConfiguration(node *model.Proxy, push *model.PushContext, extensionConfigNames []string,
    50→		pullSecrets map[string][]byte) []*core.TypedExtensionConfig
    51→
    52→	// MeshConfigChanged is invoked when mesh config is changed, giving a chance to rebuild any cached config.
    53→	MeshConfigChanged(mesh *meshconfig.MeshConfig)
    54→}
    55→
    56→type ConfigGeneratorImpl struct {
    57→	Cache model.XdsCache
    58→}
    59→
    60→func NewConfigGenerator(cache model.XdsCache) *ConfigGeneratorImpl {
    61→	return &ConfigGeneratorImpl{
    62→		Cache: cache,
    63→	}
    64→}
    65→
    66→// MeshConfigChanged is called when mesh config is changed.
    67→func (configgen *ConfigGeneratorImpl) MeshConfigChanged(_ *meshconfig.MeshConfig) {
    68→	accessLogBuilder.reset()
    69→}
    70→
