    15→package xds
    16→
    17→import (
    18→	"sync"
    19→
    20→	"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model"
    21→)
    22→
    23→type PushQueue struct {
    24→	cond *sync.Cond
    25→
    26→	// pending stores all connections in the queue. If the same connection is enqueued again,
    27→	// the PushRequest will be merged.
    28→	pending map[*Connection]*model.PushRequest
    29→
    30→	// queue maintains ordering of the queue
    31→	queue []*Connection
    32→
    33→	// processing stores all connections that have been Dequeue(), but not MarkDone().
    34→	// The value stored will be initially be nil, but may be populated if the connection is Enqueue().
    35→	// If model.PushRequest is not nil, it will be Enqueued again once MarkDone has been called.
    36→	processing map[*Connection]*model.PushRequest
    37→
    38→	shuttingDown bool
    39→}
    40→
    41→func NewPushQueue() *PushQueue {
    42→	return &PushQueue{
    43→		pending:    make(map[*Connection]*model.PushRequest),
    44→		processing: make(map[*Connection]*model.PushRequest),
    45→		cond:       sync.NewCond(&sync.Mutex{}),
    46→	}
    47→}
    48→
    49→// Enqueue will mark a proxy as pending a push. If it is already pending, pushInfo will be merged.
    50→// ServiceEntry updates will be added together, and full will be set if either were full
    51→func (p *PushQueue) Enqueue(con *Connection, pushRequest *model.PushRequest) {
    52→	p.cond.L.Lock()
    53→	defer p.cond.L.Unlock()
    54→
    55→	if p.shuttingDown {
    56→		return
    57→	}
    58→
    59→	// If its already in progress, merge the info and return
    60→	if request, f := p.processing[con]; f {
    61→		p.processing[con] = request.CopyMerge(pushRequest)
    62→		return
    63→	}
    64→
    65→	if request, f := p.pending[con]; f {
    66→		p.pending[con] = request.CopyMerge(pushRequest)
    67→		return
    68→	}
    69→
    70→	p.pending[con] = pushRequest
    71→	p.queue = append(p.queue, con)
    72→	// Signal waiters on Dequeue that a new item is available
    73→	p.cond.Signal()
    74→}
    75→
    76→// Remove a proxy from the queue. If there are no proxies ready to be removed, this will block
    77→func (p *PushQueue) Dequeue() (con *Connection, request *model.PushRequest, shutdown bool) {
    78→	p.cond.L.Lock()
    79→	defer p.cond.L.Unlock()
    80→
    81→	// Block until there is one to remove. Enqueue will signal when one is added.
    82→	for len(p.queue) == 0 && !p.shuttingDown {
    83→		p.cond.Wait()
    84→	}
    85→
    86→	if len(p.queue) == 0 {
    87→		// We must be shutting down.
    88→		return nil, nil, true
    89→	}
    90→
    91→	con = p.queue[0]
    92→	// The underlying array will still exist, despite the slice changing, so the object may not GC without this
    93→	// See https://github.com/grpc/grpc-go/issues/4758
    94→	p.queue[0] = nil
    95→	p.queue = p.queue[1:]
    96→
    97→	request = p.pending[con]
    98→	delete(p.pending, con)
    99→
   100→	// Mark the connection as in progress

    86→// typedXdsCache interface defines a store for caching XDS responses.
    87→// All operations are thread safe.
    88→type typedXdsCache[K comparable] interface {
    89→	// Flush clears the evicted indexes.

    92→	// If the cache has been updated to a newer push context, the write will be dropped silently.
    93→	// This ensures stale data does not overwrite fresh data when dealing with concurrent
    94→	// writers.
    95→	Add(key K, entry dependents, pushRequest *PushRequest, value *discovery.Resource)
    96→	// Get retrieves the cached value if it exists.
    97→	Get(key K) *discovery.Resource
    98→	// Clear removes the cache entries that are dependent on the configs passed.
    99→	Clear(sets.Set[ConfigKey])
   100→	// ClearAll clears the entire cache.

  1342→// InitContext will initialize the data structures used for code generation.
  1343→// This should be called before starting the push, from the thread creating
  1344→// the push context.
  1345→func (ps *PushContext) InitContext(env *Environment, oldPushContext *PushContext, pushReq *PushRequest) {
  1346→	// Acquire a lock to ensure we don't concurrently initialize the same PushContext.
  1347→	// If this does happen, one thread will block then exit early from InitDone=true
  1348→	ps.initializeMutex.Lock()
  1349→	defer ps.initializeMutex.Unlock()
  1350→	if ps.InitDone.Load() {
  1351→		return
  1352→	}
  1353→
  1354→	ps.Mesh = env.Mesh()
  1355→	ps.Networks = env.MeshNetworks()
  1356→
  1357→	// Must be initialized first as initServiceRegistry/VirtualServices/Destrules
  1358→	// use the default export map.
  1359→	ps.initDefaultExportMaps()
  1360→
  1361→	// create new or incremental update
  1362→	if pushReq == nil || oldPushContext == nil || !oldPushContext.InitDone.Load() || pushReq.Forced {
  1363→		ps.createNewContext(env)
  1364→	} else {
  1365→		ps.updateContext(env, oldPushContext, pushReq)
  1366→	}
  1367→
  1368→	ps.networkMgr = env.NetworkManager
  1369→
  1370→	ps.clusterLocalHosts = env.ClusterLocal().GetClusterLocalHosts()
  1371→
  1372→	ps.InitDone.Store(true)
  1373→}
  1374→
  1375→func (ps *PushContext) createNewContext(env *Environment) {
  1376→	ps.initServiceRegistry(env, nil)
  1377→
  1378→	ps.initKubernetesGateways(env)
  1379→
  1380→	ps.initVirtualServices(env)
  1381→
  1382→	ps.initDestinationRules(env)
  1383→	ps.initAuthnPolicies(env)
  1384→
  1385→	ps.initAuthorizationPolicies(env)
  1386→	ps.initTelemetry(env)
  1387→	ps.initProxyConfigs(env)
  1388→	ps.initWasmPlugins(env)
  1389→	ps.initEnvoyFilters(env, nil, nil)
  1390→	ps.initGateways(env)
  1391→	ps.initAmbient(env)
  1392→
  1393→	// Must be initialized in the end
  1394→	ps.initSidecarScopes(env)
  1395→}
  1396→
  1397→func (ps *PushContext) updateContext(
  1398→	env *Environment,
  1399→	oldPushContext *PushContext,
  1400→	pushReq *PushRequest,
  1401→) {
  1402→	var servicesChanged, virtualServicesChanged, destinationRulesChanged, gatewayChanged,
  1403→		authnChanged, authzChanged, envoyFiltersChanged, sidecarsChanged, telemetryChanged,
  1404→		wasmPluginsChanged, proxyConfigsChanged bool
  1405→
  1406→	changedEnvoyFilters := sets.New[ConfigKey]()
  1407→
  1408→	// We do not need to watch Ingress or Gateway API changes. Both of these have their own controllers which will send
  1409→	// events for Istio types (Gateway and VirtualService).
  1410→	for conf := range pushReq.ConfigsUpdated {
  1411→		switch conf.Kind {
  1412→		case kind.ServiceEntry, kind.DNSName:
  1413→			servicesChanged = true
  1414→		case kind.DestinationRule:
  1415→			destinationRulesChanged = true
  1416→		case kind.VirtualService:
  1417→			virtualServicesChanged = true
  1418→		case kind.Gateway:
  1419→			gatewayChanged = true

[metadata] {"type": "text", "file": {"filePath": "/workspace/pilot/pkg/model/push_context.go", "content": "}\n\n// InitContext will initialize the data structures used for code generation.\n// This should be called before starting the push, from the thread creating\n// the push context.\nfunc (ps *PushContext) InitContext(env *Environment, oldPushContext *PushContext, pushReq *PushRequest) {\n\t// Acquire a lock to ensure we don't concurrently initialize the same PushContext.\n\t// If this does happen, one thread will block then exit early from InitDone=true\n\tps.initializeMutex.Lock()\n\tdefer ps.initializeMutex.Unlock()\n\tif ps.InitDone.Load() {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tps.Mesh = env.Mesh()\n\tps.Networks = env.MeshNetworks()\n\n\t// Must be initialized first as initServiceRegistry/VirtualServices/Destrules\n\t// use the default export map.\n\tps.initDefaultExportMaps()\n\n\t// create new or incremental update\n\tif pushReq == nil || oldPushContext == nil || !oldPushContext.InitDone.Load() || pushReq.Forced {\n\t\tps.createNewContext(env)\n\t} else {\n\t\tps.updateContext(env, oldPushContext, pushReq)\n\t}\n\n\tps.networkMgr = env.NetworkManager\n\n\tps.clusterLocalHosts = env.ClusterLocal().GetClusterLocalHosts()\n\n\tps.InitDone.Store(true)\n}\n\nfunc (ps *PushContext) createNewContext(env *Environment) {\n\tps.initServiceRegistry(env, nil)\n\n\tps.initKubernetesGateways(env)\n\n\tps.initVirtualServices(env)\n\n\tps.initDestinationRules(env)\n\tps.initAuthnPolicies(env)\n\n\tps.initAuthorizationPolicies(env)\n\tps.initTelemetry(env)\n\tps.initProxyConfigs(env)\n\tps.initWasmPlugins(env)\n\tps.initEnvoyFilters(env, nil, nil)\n\tps.initGateways(env)\n\tps.initAmbient(env)\n\n\t// Must be initialized in the end\n\tps.initSidecarScopes(env)\n}\n\nfunc (ps *PushContext) updateContext(\n\tenv *Environment,\n\toldPushContext *PushContext,\n\tpushReq *PushRequest,\n) {\n\tvar servicesChanged, virtualServicesChanged, destinationRulesChanged, gatewayChanged,\n\t\tauthnChanged, authzChanged, envoyFiltersChanged, sidecarsChanged, telemetryChanged,\n\t\twasmPluginsChanged, proxyConfigsChanged bool\n\n\tchangedEnvoyFilters := sets.New[ConfigKey]()\n\n\t// We do not need to watch Ingress or Gateway API changes. Both of these have their own controllers which will send\n\t// events for Istio types (Gateway and VirtualService).\n\tfor conf := range pushReq.ConfigsUpdated {\n\t\tswitch conf.Kind {\n\t\tcase kind.ServiceEntry, kind.DNSName:\n\t\t\tservicesChanged = true\n\t\tcase kind.DestinationRule:\n\t\t\tdestinationRulesChanged = true\n\t\tcase kind.VirtualService:\n\t\t\tvirtualServicesChanged = true\n\t\tcase kind.Gateway:\n\t\t\tgatewayChanged = true\n\t\tcase kind.Sidecar:\n\t\t\tsidecarsChanged = true\n\t\tcase kind.WasmPlugin:\n\t\t\twasmPluginsChanged = true\n\t\tcase kind.EnvoyFilter:\n\t\t\tenvoyFiltersChanged = true\n\t\t\tchangedEnvoyFilters.Insert(conf)\n\t\tcase kind.AuthorizationPolicy:\n\t\t\tauthzChanged = true\n\t\tcase kind.RequestAuthentication,\n\t\t\tkind.PeerAuthentication:\n\t\t\tauthnChanged = true\n\t\tcase kind.Telemetry:\n\t\t\ttelemetryChanged = true\n\t\tcase kind.ProxyConfig:\n\t\t\tproxyConfigsChanged = true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif servicesChanged {\n\t\t// Services have changed. initialize service registry\n\t\tps.initServiceRegistry(env, pushReq.ConfigsUpdated)\n\t} else {\n\t\t// make sure we copy over things that would be generated in initServiceRegistry\n\t\tps.ServiceIndex = oldPushContext.ServiceIndex\n\t\tps.serviceAccounts = oldPushContext.serviceAccounts\n\t}\n\n\tif servicesChanged {\n\t\t// Gateway status depends on services, so recompute if they change as well\n\t\tps.initKubernetesGateways(env)\n\t} else {\n\t\tps.GatewayAPIController = oldPushContext.GatewayAPIController\n\t}\n\n\tif virtualServicesChanged {\n\t\tps.initVirtualServices(env)\n\t} else {\n\t\tps.virtualServiceIndex = oldPushContext.virtualServiceIndex\n\t}", "numLines": 120, "startLine": 1340, "totalLines": 2642}}

    73→	}

During\n\t\t// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.\n\t\t// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a\n\t\t// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.\n\t\tfirstRequest.Store(false)\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server warmup not complete; try again\")\n\t}\n\t// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.\n\t// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build\n\t// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would\n\t// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing\n\t// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding\n\t// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy\n\t// ip tables update latencies.\n\t// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.\n\tif !s.IsServerReady() {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"server is not ready to serve discovery information\")\n\t}\n\n\tctx := stream.Context()\n\tpeerAddr := \"0.0.0.0\"\n\tif peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {\n\t\tpeerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()\n\t}\n\n\tif err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {\n\t\tdeltaLog.Warnf(\"ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v\", peerAddr, err)\n\t\treturn status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, \"request rate limit exceeded: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())\n\t}\n\tif ids != nil {\n\t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v\", peerAddr, ids)", "numLines": 80, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 685}}
